structured.description: A `Camera` class that abstracts away the complexity of representing a 3D camera in a 2D viewport. It provides a `getViewProjection()` method that returns the matrix for the camera's view and projection, which can be used to render the camera's view in the 2D viewport. The class also provides methods for calculating the view matrix and translating the camera position. Additionally, it extends the `CameraStruct` class, which is an abstract class that provides a way to calculate the projection matrix for the camera.

items.description: Is an abstract class that defines various methods and fields for manipulating camera perspectives in a 3D environment. It has an associated projection matrix and view projection matrix, as well as a transformation matrix for adjusting the position and rotation of the camera. Additionally, it has a getTranslationMatrix method for calculating the translation matrix of the camera, and an adjustToViewport method for adjusting the camera to the specified viewport size. The class also includes an abstract calculateProjectionMatrix method and an abstract class CameraStruct for storing camera-related data.
items.name: Camera

items.description: Calculates and returns a matrix representation of the view-projection transformation, based on the current view and projection matrices.

returns.description: a `Matrix4f` object representing the view projection matrix.

items.name: getViewProjection

items.description: Calculates a view matrix by multiplying the camera's rotation and translation matrices with the projection matrix.

returns.description: a matrix representation of the view transformation, which combines the rotation and translation of the camera.

usage.description: This code initializes a projection and view matrix, then creates a new instance of the `Camera` class with the projection matrix. It then sets the view matrix to identity and calculates the view matrix using the `calculateViewMatrix()` method. Finally, it prints the resulting view matrix.

items.name: calculateViewMatrix

items.description: Generates a matrix that represents the translation of a 3D camera's position from its original location to a new location. The matrix is initialized with the camera's current position and then translated by a negative value to represent the new position.

returns.description: a 4x4 matrix representing the translation of the camera position from its original location.

items.name: getTranslationMatrix

items.description: Returns the `transform` object, which is an instance of the `Transform` class. This object contains information about the position and orientation of an object in a 3D space.

returns.description: a reference to an instance of the `Transform` class.

usage.description: In this example, a new perspective camera object is created and the getTransform() method is called to retrieve its transform property. The returned Transform object is then printed to the console.

items.name: getTransform

items.description: Is an abstract class that serves as a base for other classes in the Camera package. It has an abstract method called getAsMatrix4() that returns a Matrix4f object, but it does not provide any implementation details. The class provides a way for subclasses to inherit and implement their own matrix manipulation methods without having to write their own abstract methods.
items.name: CameraStruct

structured.description: A 3D camera class `Camera3D` that extends the basic `Camera` class. The camera can be initialized with different field of view (fov), aspect ratio, near and far distances, and then various methods are called to calculate the projection matrix, adjust to the viewport size, process input from mouse and keyboard, and move the camera position.

items.description: Is an extension of the Camera class that provides additional functionality for manipulating the camera's position and orientation in 3D space. The class has several methods for calculating and adjusting the camera's projection matrix, as well as processing input from the user. These include move(), calculateProjectionMatrix(), and adjustToViewport().
items.name: Camera3D

items.description: Calculates a matrix representing the projection of a 3D scene from a specified viewpoint, based on the data provided by the `CameraStruct` parameter.

params.name: data
params.description: 3D camera's projection matrix.

returns.description: a Matrix4f object representing the projection matrix.

usage.description: In this example, the method takes in a CameraStruct object as an argument. The method then sets the projection field to the projection matrix returned by the getAsMatrix4() method of the CameraStruct object. This is done using the assignment operator (=).

items.name: calculateProjectionMatrix

items.description: Adjusts the camera's projection matrix and view matrix to fit within the given viewport dimensions, and then sets the viewport size using `GL11.glViewport()`.

params.name: width
params.description: 2D viewport width for which the projection and view matrices are calculated.

params.name: height
params.description: 2D viewport dimensions, which are used to calculate the aspect ratio and projection matrix of the 3D scene.

usage.description:
Explanation:
The camera object, which extends the Camera class, has a method adjustToViewport that is overwritten in the child class Camera3D to account for 2D projection. This method takes two inputs, width and height, which are the dimensions of the viewport that the camera will render to. The method first calculates the aspect ratio by dividing the width by the height, and then calls the calculateProjectionMatrix method on the values property, which is an instance of the CameraStruct3D class. This method returns a projection matrix that accounts for the aspect ratio, field of view (FOV), near plane, and far plane of the camera. Next, the try block calculates the view matrix, but if any exceptions occur, such as NullPointerException, it is handled by the catch block. Finally, the GL11 class calls the glViewport method to set the viewport to the dimensions specified in the input.

It is important to note that this code is not meant to be executed on its own but rather serve as an example of how the adjustToViewport method would work with the given inputs and implementation.

items.name: adjustToViewport

items.description: Is an extension of the Camera Struct class that adds additional parameters to represent the field of view (fov), aspect ratio, near and far distances, and provides a method to get the matrix representation of the camera's perspective projection.
items.name: CameraStruct3D

items.description: Initializes a `Matrix4f` object with a perspective projection, combining the field of view (fov), aspect ratio, near and far distances.

returns.description: a 4x4 matrix representing a perspective projection.

items.name: getAsMatrix4

items.description: Processes mouse and keyboard input to move an object based on sensitivity and speed, rotates the object based on the mouse input, and implements basic movement using the W, S, A, and D keys.

params.name: dt
params.description: time step of the simulation, which is multiplied with the movement speed to determine the amount of movement per frame.

params.name: speed
params.description: 3D movement speed of the object being manipulated by the function, which is multiplied by the time elapsed (represented by `dt`) to determine the total distance traveled during the specified time interval.

params.name: sens
params.description: sensitivity of the mouse input, which determines how much the character will move based on the user's mouse input.

usage.description: This method would be called every time the user wants to move or rotate the camera. The dt variable would represent the time since the last frame and is used for calculating movement speed. The speed variable would be the actual movement speed and the sens variable would be the sensitivity of the rotation.

items.name: processInput

items.description: Moves an object by a specified distance in a particular direction using a transform's position property.

params.name: dir
params.description: 3D direction in which to move the object, with the direction being multiplied by the specified amount (`amt`).

params.name: amt
params.description: amount of movement to apply to the object's position, as measured in the direction specified by the `dir` parameter.

usage.description: This code creates a Camera3D object with the default constructor and sets its position to (0, 0, -10). Then it defines a direction vector (0, 0, -1) and an amount of movement (10). The move method is called on myCamera with these parameters, which will move the camera in the specified direction by the specified amount.

items.name: move

structured.description: A matrix4f class that represents a 4x4 matrix in the form (a, b, c, d) where a, b, c, and d are the elements of the matrix. The class provides methods for setting and getting the elements of the matrix, as well as methods for transforming vectors using the matrix. Additionally, the code defines several static methods for initializing matrices with different orientations and positions, as well as methods for multiplying the matrix by another matrix.

items.description: Is a representation of a 4x4 homogeneous transformation matrix. It has several methods for setting and getting values of its elements, as well as methods for multiplying it with other matrices, transforming vectors, and getting the linear data of the matrix.
items.name: Matrix4f

items.description: Initializes a matrix to the identity matrix, with all elements set to either 0 or 1.

returns.description:
a Matrix4f object with identity matrix elements.

The output is a `Matrix4f` object representing an identity matrix with 4x4 dimensions. The elements of the matrix are set to their default values, which are:

* Data[0][0] = 1
* Data[0][1] = 0
* Data[0][2] = 0
* Data[0][3] = 0
* Data[1][0] = 0
* Data[1][1] = 1
* Data[1][2] = 0
* Data[1][3] = 0
* Data[2][0] = 0
* Data[2][1] = 0
* Data[2][2] = 1
* Data[2][3] = 0
* Data[3][0] = 0
* Data[3][1] = 0
* Data[3][2] = 0
* Data[3][3] = 1

In summary, the `initIdentity` function returns an identity matrix with 4x4 dimensions, where each element is set to its default value.

usage.code:
Matrix4f matrix = new Matrix4f();
matrix.initIdentity().transform(new Vector3f(1, 2, 3)); //returns a vector with x=1, y=2, z=3
usage.description:
This example initializes an empty matrix and uses the method initIdentity to set all of its values to their respective identity values. The matrix is then used to transform a new Vector3f object with the values (1, 2, 3). The output of this transformation would be a vector with the same x, y, and z components.

a matrix that represents the view transformation for a perspective projection.

usage.description:
In this example, the float variables fov, aspectRatio, and zNear are provided as inputs to the initPerspective method. The displayWidth and displayHeight are variables representing the dimensions of the viewport being used to render the scene. These variables are set by the user and are used to compute the aspect ratio. The zNear variable is the distance from the eye point where the near plane of the clipping volume starts, while the zFar variable is the distance from the eye point where the far plane of the clipping volume ends. This method sets the member variable data for a matrix that represents a perspective projection transformation in 3D graphics.

The initPerspective method first computes the tangent of half the field of view using the Math.tan(float) method, and then uses this value to set the first three values of the first row in the Matrix4f object's data array. The first column is initialized to 0 for all elements, as a perspective projection transformation does not involve any scaling along the X axis.

The second row in the data array is initialized with the tangent of half the field of view divided by the aspect ratio, and then set to zero for all columns. This ensures that the Y axis scaling factor is 1 while the X axis scaling factor is computed based on the aspect ratio of the viewport.

The third row in the data array is initialized with values representing the difference between zNear and zFar divided by the product of the two, and then set to zero for all columns. This sets the Z axis scaling factor to 1 while also computing the perspective divide (z = zFar * zNear / (z - zNear)) to map the near plane to the far plane.

Finally, the fourth row in the data array is initialized to zeros for all elements, as this transformation does not involve any translation along the Z axis. This method returns a Matrix4f object that contains the computed perspective projection transformation values for use in 3D graphics calculations.

items.description: Provides a set of methods for working with 2D vectors in a mathematical context. It has fields for storing the x and y components of the vector, and various methods for performing operations such as length, max, dot product, normalization, cross product, lerping, rotating, adding and subtracting other vectors or scalars, multiplying by other vectors, dividing by other vectors, and more. Additionally, it provides a method for converting itself to a 3D vector and offers getters and setters for the x and y components.
items.name: Vector2f

items.description: Calculates the Euclidean distance between a point and the origin, taking into account both the x and y coordinates.

returns.description: the square root of the sum of the squares of the x and y coordinates.

usage.code:
Vector2f vector = new Vector2f(3, 4);
float length = vector.length(); // Output: 5

items.name: length

items.description: Calculates the maximum value of two input values `x` and `y`, using the `Math.max()` method.

returns.description: the larger of the input values `x` and `y`.

usage.code:
Vector2f v = new Vector2f(10, 5);
float maxValue = v.max(); // Output: 10

items.name: max

items.description: Computes the dot product of a `Vector2f` object and a given `Vector2f` argument, returning the result as a float value.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to which the `x` and `y` components of the returned value should be dot-producted.

returns.description: a scalar value representing the dot product of the input vector and another vector.

items.name: dot

items.description: Normalizes a `Vector2f` object by dividing its components by their corresponding lengths.

returns.description: a normalized version of the input vector.

items.name: normalized

items.description: Computes the dot product of two vectors and returns the result as a single float value, with the x-component of the first vector multiplied by the y-component of the second vector and vice versa.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to be crossed with the current vector, resulting in a new 2D vector output.

returns.description: a floating-point number representing the dot product of two vectors.

items.name: cross

items.description: Computes a vector interpolating between two given vectors using linear interpolation. The input vectors are combined to form the destination vector, and the resulting vector is returned.

params.name: dest
params.description: 2D destination vector that the `lerp` method will interpolate between the `this` parameter and the given `lerpFactor`.

params.name: lerpFactor
params.description: amount of linear interpolation between the starting and ending positions of the vector, with values between 0 and 1 resulting in a smooth transition.

returns.description: a vector that interpolates between the original and destination vectors using the provided lerp factor.

items.name: lerp

items.description: Takes a float angle and returns a new `Vector2f` instance with its x-coordinate rotated by the angle and its y-coordinate unchanged.

params.name: angle
params.description: angle of rotation in radians, which is used to calculate the x and y components of the output vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` instance with its x-component rotated by the specified angle and its y-component unaffected.

usage.description: The method takes a single argument 'angle', which is a float representing the rotation angle in degrees. The method first converts the angle from degrees to radians using Math.toRadians(). It then calculates the cosine and sine of the angle using the Math.cos() and Math.sin() methods, respectively. Finally, it returns a new Vector2f object with the rotated coordinates using the formula: x' = x * cos(θ) - y * sin(θ), y' = x * sin(θ) + y * cos(θ).

items.name: rotate

items.description: Adds a vector to the current vector. It takes another vector as input and returns a new vector representing the sum of both vectors.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to be added to the current vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` object representing the sum of the input vectors.

items.name: add

items.description: Takes a floating-point value `r` and returns a new `Vector2f` instance representing the sum of the current vector's components plus `r`.

params.name: r
params.description: addition to be applied to the `x` and `y` coordinates of the vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` instance representing the sum of the original vector and the input float value.

usage.code:
Vector2f vector1 = new Vector2f(3, 4);
Vector2f result = vector1.add(5); //result will be a Vector2f object with x=8 and y=9

items.name: add

items.description: Adds two floating-point values to a `Vector2f` object, returning a new `Vector2f` instance with the sum of the original's x and y components and the input x and y values.

params.name: x
params.description: 2D coordinate to be added to the current position of the vector.

params.name: y
params.description: 2nd component of the resulting vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` instance representing the sum of the input `x` and `y` values.

items.name: add

items.description: Takes a `Vector2f` argument `r` and returns a new `Vector2f` representing the difference between the input vector and the reference vector.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to be subtracted from the current vector.

returns.description: a new vector representing the difference between the input vector and the reference vector.

usage.description: In this example, a vector is created using the constructor and its components are initialized to 3 and 4 respectively. The method `sub()` is then called on the first vector with another vector as an argument, which results in a new vector with x=2 and y=2. Finally, the result of the subtraction is printed to the console.

items.name: sub

items.description: Subtracts a given scalar value `r` from a `Vector2f` object's `x` and `y` components, returning a new `Vector2f` object with the differences.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector offset to subtract from the original vector's components, resulting in the new vector output.

returns.description: a vector with the difference between the original vector's coordinates and the given value.

items.name: sub

items.description: Multiplies its input `Vector2f` by a given scalar value and returns the result as a new `Vector2f`.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector that multiplies with the current vector, resulting in a new 2D vector.

returns.description: a vector with the product of the input vectors' x and y components.

usage.code:
Vector2f vectorA = new Vector2f(10, 5); // x-coordinate: 10 y-coordinate: 5
Vector2f vectorB = new Vector2f(3, 7); // x-coordinate: 3 y-coordinate: 7
Vector2f result = vectorA.mul(vectorB); // result is (30, 35)

items.name: mul

items.description: Multiplies its input vector by a scalar value, returning a new vector with the product.

params.name: r
params.description: scaling factor applied to the `Vector2f` instance being multiplied.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` instance with the product of the component values of the input vector and the scalar value `r`.

items.name: mul

items.description: Takes a `Vector2f` argument `r` and returns a new `Vector2f` object with the x-coordinate calculated as `x / r.getX()` and the y-coordinate calculated as `y / r.getY()`.

params.name: r
params.description: vector that the current vector will be divided by.

returns.description: a vector with the same x-coordinate as the original vector, scaled by the reciprocal of the input vector's x-coordinate.

items.name: div

items.description: Divides the components of a `Vector2f` object by a given scalar value, resulting in a new `Vector2f` object with scaled components.

params.name: r
params.description: scalar value that the vector is to be divided by.

returns.description: a vector with x and y components scaled by the input parameter `r`.

usage.code:
Vector2f v1 = new Vector2f(5, 10); // create a vector with x value 5 and y value 10
float r = 2; // the divisor
v1.div(r); // return a new vector with values 2.5 and 5.0
usage.description: In this example, we first create a `Vector2f` object `v1` with x value 5 and y value 10. Then we create a float variable `r` with the value of 2. Finally, we use the method `div` to divide both values in the vector by the divisor `r`, returning a new vector with values 2.5 for x and 5.0 for y.

items.name: div

items.description: Computes and returns a vector with absolute values of its x and y components.

returns.description: a new `Vector2f` object containing the absolute values of the input vector's components.

usage.code:
Vector2f vector = new Vector2f(3, 4);
vector.abs(); // returns the absolute value of the vector (5, 5)

items.name: abs

items.description: Returns a string representation of a point (x,y) by concatenating the values of x and y in parentheses.

returns.description: a string representation of the current point in the form (x, y).

items.name: toString

items.description: Updates the `x` and `y` components of the `Vector2f` object, returning a reference to the same object for chaining calls.

params.name: x
params.description: 2D vector's x-coordinate, which is being set to the provided value.

params.name: y
params.description: 2D coordinate value that will be set for the `Vector2f` object.

returns.description: a reference to the modified `Vector2f` instance.

usage.code:
Vector2f vector = new Vector2f(1, 1);
vector.set(-3.0, -4.5); // Sets the x-coordinate to -3.0 and y-coordinate to -4.5

items.name: set

items.description: Sets the x and y components of the object to the corresponding values of the provided `Vector2f` object.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to be set as the value of the `this` vector.

returns.description: a reference to the original `Vector2f` object, unchanged.

usage.description: In this example, we create two `Vector2f` objects, `vec1` and `vec2`, with different values for their coordinates. We then use the `set()` method to assign the coordinates of `vec2` to `vec1`. Finally, we print out the value of `vec1` using its `toString()` method, which prints `(3 4)`.

items.name: set

items.description: Converts a `Vector2f` object to a `Vector3f` object by adding a third component representing the z-coordinate, which is set to zero by default.

returns.description: a new `Vector3f` instance with the values `x`, `y`, and `0`.

items.name: as3DVector

items.description: Retrieves the value of the `x` field, which is a `float` variable representing the X-coordinate of an object or point.

returns.description: the value of `x`, which is a float variable.

usage.description: In this code, a vector with coordinates (1,2) is created and the value of its x-coordinate is retrieved using the getX() method. This value is then printed to the console.

items.name: getX

items.description: Sets the value of the `x` field of its object parameter to the provided float value.

params.name: x
params.description: float value that will be assigned to the `x` field of the class object.

usage.description: In this example, we create a new instance of the Vector2f class called myVector and set its coordinates to (1,1). Then, we call the setX() method with an input of 2, which updates the x-coordinate of myVector. Finally, we print out the updated value of the x-coordinate using the getX() method, which should output 2.

items.name: setX

items.description: Returns the value of the `y` field.

returns.description: the value of the `y` field, which is a `float` variable.

items.name: getY

items.description: Sets the value of the `y` field of its receiver to the argument passed as a float.

params.name: y
params.description: vertical position of an object or point in a two-dimensional space, and it is assigned to the field `y` of the class.

usage.description: The example code uses the setY method to change the y value of the vector2f object from its original value of 1 to the new value of 2.

items.name: setY

items.description: Compares a `Vector2f` object to another, returning `true` if the object's `x` and `y` values match those of the compared object, otherwise `false`.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector to be compared with the current vector for equality.

returns.description: a boolean value indicating whether the vector's x and y coordinates are equal to those of the provided vector.

items.name: equals

structured.description: A `Vector3f` class with various methods for mathematical operations on 3D vectors, including addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and more. The class also provides functions for calculating the length, square length, maximum value, dot product, cross product, normalization, rotation, and lerp.

items.description: Is a mathematical representation of a three-dimensional point in space. It has several fields and methods for performing various calculations related to vectors, such as addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division by other vectors or scalars. The class also provides methods for calculating the magnitude (length) of a vector, normalizing it, rotating it, and converting it between different coordinate systems. Additionally, it has a string representation method for converting the vector to a String.
items.name: Vector3f

items.description: Calculates the Euclidean distance of a point from its origin using the Pythagorean theorem and square root operation.

returns.description: the square root of the sum of the squares of the coordinates.

items.name: length

items.description: Calculates the square of a 3D vector's components and returns the result as a float value.

returns.description: a floating-point number representing the square of the length of the provided vector.

items.name: squareLength

items.description: Computes the maximum value of three arguments: `x`, `y`, and `z`. It returns the maximum value using the `Math.max()` method.

returns.description: the maximum value of `x`, `y`, and `z`.

usage.description: This code creates an instance of a vector and calls the max method on it to get the maximum value among its three components. The result is then printed to standard output.

items.name: max

items.description: Computes the dot product of a `Vector3f` object and another vector.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector to be dot-producted with the `x`, `y`, and `z` components of the function's output.

returns.description: a scalar value representing the dot product of the vector `r` and the object's position vector.

items.name: dot

items.description: Takes a `Vector3f` argument `r` and returns a new `Vector3f` object with the dot product of the two vectors as its components.

params.name: r
params.description: 2D vector that the `cross()` method computes the cross product of with the given 3D vector.

returns.description: a vector with the cross product of the input vectors.

items.name: cross

items.description: Normalizes a `Vector3f` instance by dividing each component by its magnitude, resulting in a unitized vector representation.

returns.description: a normalized vector in the form of a `Vector3f` instance, where the magnitude of the vector is equal to 1.

usage.description: The example code creates an instance of the class Vector3f with values 1, 1 and 1. The normalized method is then called on the vector which normalizes it. Afterwards, the assertEquals function checks if the result of the call to the normalized method is equal to a new instance of Vector3f which has values of (0.577, 0.577, 0.577). If they are not equal, the test will fail.

items.name: normalized

items.description: Rotates a 3D vector by an angle around a specified axis, returning the resultant vector.

params.name: axis
params.description: 3D vector that defines the rotation axis.

params.name: angle
params.description: 3D rotation angle of the vector, which is used to calculate the resulting rotated vector.

returns.description: a rotated version of the original vector, based on the provided axis and angle of rotation.

items.name: rotate

items.description: Rotates a `Vector3f` object by the specified `Quaternion` rotation, resulting in a new `Vector3f` object with the rotated components.

params.name: rotation
params.description: 3D rotation matrix that is applied to the `Vector3f` object.

returns.description: a vector representing the rotated position of the original vector.

items.name: rotate

items.description: Interpolates a destination vector (`dest`) towards a starting vector (`this`), based on a lerp factor `lerpFactor`. The function returns the interpolated vector as the result.

params.name: dest
params.description: 3D destination vector to which the current vector will be interpolated.

params.name: lerpFactor
params.description: amount of interpolation or blending between the starting and ending points of the vector, with higher values resulting in more gradual transitions.

returns.description: a new vector that is a linear combination of the input vectors, with the specified lerp factor.

items.name: lerp

items.description: Adds two vector objects, returning a new vector object with the sum of the x, y, and z components of each vector.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector to be added to the current vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector3f` instance with the sum of the input vectors' coordinates.

usage.description: This code creates two Vector3f objects with the values of (2, 4, 6) and (1, 2, 3). The first Vector3f object is then added to the second object using the add() method, resulting in a new Vector3f object with the value (3, 6, 9). This result is then printed to the console using the println() method.

items.name: add

items.description: Updates the instance's position by adding the specified vector's components to its own.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector that adds its components to the current object's position.

items.name: addSelf

items.description: Adds a scalar value `r` to the components of its input vector, returning a new vector with the added value.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector to be added to the existing vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector3f` object representing the sum of the original vector and the specified float value.

usage.code:
Vector3f v = new Vector3f(); // initialize vector object
v.add(2.0); // adds the scalar value 2.0 to each element of the vector, creating a new vector with modified values.

items.name: add

items.description: Adds a vector to the current position, scaling the input vector by a provided factor beforehand. The resulting vector is then returned.

params.name: v
params.description: 3D vector to be added to the current vector, multiplied by a scalar value.

params.name: scale
params.description: scalar value that is multiplied with the `v` input parameter, resulting in the new vector sum.

returns.description: a new vector that is the result of adding the original vector multiplied by the scale factor.

items.name: addScaledVector

items.description: Multiplies a `Vector3f` object by a scalar value and adds it to the current vector representation of the object, scaling the vector's components accordingly.

params.name: v
params.description: 3D vector to be scaled.

params.name: scale
params.description: 3D vector that is to be multiplied with the original `Vector3f` value.

items.name: addSelfScaledVector

items.description: Takes a `Vector3f` argument `r` and returns a new `Vector3f` object representing the difference between the input and the current object's values.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector to be subtracted from the current vector, resulting in the new vector.

returns.description: a new `Vector3f` instance representing the difference between the input vector and the reference vector.

usage.code:
Vector3f v1 = new Vector3f(2, 5, 7); // x = 2, y = 5, z = 7
Vector3f v2 = new Vector3f(4, 2, 8); // x = 4, y = 2, z = 8

v1.sub(r).toString(); // prints "(2 - 4, 5 - 2, 7 - 8)"

items.name: sub

items.description: Takes a single float argument `r` and returns a new `Vector3f` instance representing the difference between the original vector and `r`.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D position from which to subtract the output of the function.

returns.description: a vector representing the difference between the original vector and the given value.

items.name: sub

items.description: Takes a `Vector3f` object `r` and multiplies its components by the corresponding components of the current vector, returning a new vector with the result.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector to be multiplied with the current vector, resulting in a new 3D vector output.

returns.description: a vector with the product of the input vectors' coordinates.

items.name: mul

items.description: Multiplies the components of a `Vector3f` object by a scalar value `r`. The returned `Vector3f` object has the same x, y, and z components as the original, but with the values scaled by `r`.

params.name: r
params.description: scalar value that is to be multiplied with the vector components of the `Vector3f` object.

returns.description: a vector with components scaled by the input parameter `r`.

items.name: mul

items.description: Takes a reference to another vector and returns a new vector with the components scaled by the reciprocal of the corresponding values of the provided vector.

params.name: r
params.description: vector to divide by, and it is used to calculate the output vector.

returns.description: a vector with the same components as the input vector, but scaled by the reciprocal of the corresponding component of the argument vector.

usage.description:
This code creates a vector object named "vector" and a vector object named "r". The "result" object is then set to the division of the "vector" by "r".

Therefore, the output of this program would be: 
The result would have coordinates (1/10, 1/20, 1/30).

items.name: div

items.description: Takes a scalar value `r` and returns a new `Vector3f` instance with components scaled by the reciprocal of `r`.

params.name: r
params.description: scalar value used to divide each component of the `Vector3f` object.

returns.description: a vector with its x, y, and z components scaled by the input parameter `r`.

usage.code:
Vector3f a = new Vector3f(1, 2, 3);
a.div(2); // returns (0.5, 1, 1.5)

items.name: div

items.description: Computes and returns a new `Vector3f` object containing the absolute values of its input components.

returns.description: a new vector with the absolute value of the original vector's components.

usage.description: In this example, the vector `vector` is initialized with negative values and then the `abs()` method is called to get an absolute version of the vector. The resulting vector has positive values for all components.

items.name: abs

items.description: Returns a string representation of an object, including its instance variables (x, y, and z) separated by spaces.

returns.description: a string representation of a point in 3D space, consisting of three values separated by spaces.

items.name: toString

items.description: Returns a `Vector2f` object containing the x and y coordinates of a point.

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object containing the `x` and `y` coordinates of the point.

items.name: getXY

items.description: Returns a `Vector2f` object representing the y- and z-components of a point.

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object containing the values of `y` and `z`.

items.name: getYZ

items.description: Returns a new `Vector2f` object representing the point (x, z).

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object representing the coordinates (x, z).

items.name: getZX

items.description: Creates a new `Vector2f` object and returns its values for `y` and `x`.

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object representing the coordinate values of the point (x, y).

usage.description: In this example, we create a vector v1 and then use the getYX method to extract the components of the vector in a Vector2f object. We then print out the result.

items.name: getYX

items.description: Returns a `Vector2f` object containing the `z` and `y` components of a point.

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object representing the coordinate (z, y) of a point.

usage.code:
Vector3f v = new Vector3f(1, 2, 3);
Vector2f zy = v.getZY();

items.name: getZY

items.description: Returns a `Vector2f` object containing the `x` and `z` coordinates of a point.

returns.description: a `Vector2f` object containing the x and z coordinates of the point.

usage.code:
Vector3f vector = new Vector3f(1, 2, 3);
Vector2f xz = vector.getXZ();
usage.description: This will return a new Vector2f object with values of (1, 3).

items.name: getXZ

items.description: Sets the values of the `x`, `y`, and `z` fields of a `Vector3f` object to the input parameters.

params.name: x
params.description: 3D position of the vector along the x-axis.

params.name: y
params.description: 2D position of the vector in the Y-axis.

params.name: z
params.description: 3D position of the object along the z-axis.

returns.description: a reference to the modified `Vector3f` object.

items.name: set

items.description: Sets the position of a vector to the given coordinates.

params.name: r
params.description: 3D vector that sets the values of the `Vector3f` object.

returns.description: a reference to the same `Vector3f` object, unchanged.

items.name: set

items.description: Retrieves the value of the `x` field.

returns.description: the value of the `x` field.

items.name: getX

items.description: Sets the value of the `x` field of its object to the input float value.

params.name: x
params.description: float value that will be assigned to the `x` field of the class instance being passed as an argument to the `setX()` method.

usage.code:
Vector3f vector = new Vector3f(); //create a new instance of the class 
vector.setX(5);                   //sets x to 5 and leaves y and z unchanged.

items.name: setX

items.description: Retrieves and returns the value of the `y` field of a Java object.

returns.description: a float value representing the y-coordinate of a point.

items.name: getY

items.description: Sets the value of a object's `y` field to the input float value.

params.name: y
params.description: vertical component of an object's position and stores it as the field `y`.

items.name: setY

items.description: Returns the value of the `z` field.

returns.description: a floating-point representation of the value `z`.

usage.code:
Vector3f myVector = new Vector3f(1, 2, 3);
float z = myVector.getZ(); // will return the value 3

items.name: getZ

items.description: Sets the value of the field `z` to the provided float parameter.

params.name: z
params.description: 3D position of an object in the `setZ` function.

usage.code:
Vector3f vector = new Vector3f();
vector.setZ(5); // Set the z-coordinate to 5
float z = vector.getZ(); // Get the current value of the z-coordinate
System.out.println(z); // Output: 5

items.name: setZ

items.description: Compares a vector with another vector by checking if its x, y and z components are equal to those of the other vector.

params.name: r
